page: Amazon Web Services m7g.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 CPU running at 2.6 GHz. The processor allocates a dedicated thread per core with no hyperthreading. Cache configuration includes 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7g.metal demonstrates strong multi-core performance, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and the top 10% for Geekbench multi-core. Software compilation with Clang also ranks in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with DDR5 memory achieving top-tier PassMark memory scores and strong read/write bandwidth on large uncached blocks. Single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, falls into the average category. Cryptographic operations are supported via AES hardware acceleration, though the architecture lacks AVX, AMX, and other x86-specific instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration provides direct hardware access without virtualization overhead, maximizing the utility of the 64 dedicated physical cores. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads. However, the absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. The lack of GPU acceleration also limits its utility for intensive parallel computing or large-scale AI training, though it handles small to medium LLM inference at average speeds.

This server is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and Redis deployments, where its strong memory bandwidth and multi-core capabilities are fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture. Powered by AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processors running at 2.6 GHz, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with Geekbench multi-core and software compilation scores reaching the top 10%, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth. Single-core performance remains average. The processor features 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AES cryptography, but lacks AVX or AMX extensions. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency by providing direct hardware access and high memory throughput without hyperthreading overhead, making it suited for software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and Redis deployments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 arm64 physical cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance.

tagline: A bare-metal arm64 server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and DDR5 memory for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical arm64 cores with no hyperthreading
Top-tier multi-core and software compilation benchmark scores
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for database operations, Redis, and static web serving
